privileged_info_summary: This task uses NextGen Connect (a real HL7 integration engine from NextGen Healthcare) to create a channel named 'Registration_Pipeline' that listens on TCP port 6665 using MLLP protocol. It processes synthetic HL7 ADT A01 messages (version 2.3), extracting visit_number (PV1.19) and patient_name (PID.5) fields to store in a PostgreSQL database table 'billing_log' with visit_number as primary key, then conditionally writes raw messages to /tmp/billing_out if the database insert succeeds. Database connection uses jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb with credentials postgres/postgres. All data is synthetically generated within the task framework (no real patient data). The verification logic tests that the File Writer destination waits for the Database Writer's success before executing, ensuring transactional integrity.
